page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom 64-core AWS Graviton processors built on the ARM64 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz, the instance features 64 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 512 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Local storage is bundled via 3800 GB of high-speed NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity is supported by a 30 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8gd.16xlarge demonstrates exceptional memory performance, achieving top-tier results in memory bandwidth tests with read-only small block cached speeds of 7396.42 GB/sec and large block uncached read speeds of 464.12 GB/sec. Multi-threaded processing is highly efficient, as evidenced by top-tier scores in Geekbench multi-core and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Database operations are a primary strength, with Passmark database operations scoring in the top tier and Redis SET operations reaching 134.09 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture delivers high resource density and memory throughput per unit of power, making it an efficient alternative to traditional x86 architectures for memory-intensive workloads. However, the lack of AVX instruction support and lower single-threaded performance relative to some high-frequency x86 processors represent notable tradeoffs. This instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive analytical workloads that benefit from both massive memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton processor utilizing the Neoverse-V2 architecture at 2.8 GHz. It features 512 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and 30 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. The instance delivers top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-core processing, and database operations, as demonstrated by its high scores in Geekbench multi-core and Redis SET benchmarks. By utilizing dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, it ensures consistent compute performance. The combination of high-capacity memory and fast local storage makes this instance highly efficient for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and data processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 64 physical Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance with 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 instance combining massive memory bandwidth, dedicated physical cores, and fast local NVMe storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture.
512 GB of system memory delivering top-tier read and write bandwidth.
Bundled 3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Excellent performance for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and high-throughput web serving.
